name: deploy-model
description: Unified Azure OpenAI model deployment skill with intelligent intent-based routing. Handles quick preset deployments, fully customized deployments (version/SKU/capacity/RAI policy), and capacity discovery across regions and projects. USE FOR: deploy model, deploy gpt, create deployment, model deployment, deploy openai model, set up model, provision model, find capacity, check model availability, where can I deploy, best region for model, capacity analysis. DO NOT USE FOR: listing existing deployments (use foundry_models_deployments_list MCP tool), deleting deployments, agent creation (use agent/create), project creation (use project/create).
capabilities: Deploy OpenAI models using quick preset configurations
Deploy models with custom settings (version, SKU, capacity, RAI policies)
Discover available capacity across Azure regions and projects
Query model availability in specific regions
Generate deployment URLs for Azure resources
Intelligent intent-based routing between deployment modes
author: microsoft
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 475 potential issues, but evaluation confirms these are false positives or expected behavior. The skill uses shell commands to invoke Azure CLI for model deployment operations, which is legitimate for Azure tooling. Network access is to Azure API endpoints. Filesystem patterns are documentation examples, not vulnerabilities. This is an official Microsoft skill for Azure Foundry.
valueStatement: Deploy OpenAI models to Azure Foundry with intelligent routing. This skill handles preset deployments, custom configurations, and capacity discovery across Azure regions.
limitations: Requires valid Azure subscription with access to Azure Foundry
Cannot list existing deployments (use dedicated MCP tool)
Cannot delete deployments or manage existing resources
Does not support agent or project creation

useCases.targetUser: Developers who need quick access to AI models
useCases.title: Quick Model Deployment
useCases.description: Deploy an OpenAI model with default settings for rapid prototyping

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise users with specific compliance requirements
useCases.title: Custom Enterprise Deployment
useCases.description: Configure deployment with specific SKU, capacity, and RAI policies for production

useCases.title: Capacity Discovery
useCases.description: Find available regions and capacity for specific model types before deployment

promptTemplates.title: Quick Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy gpt-4o to Azure Foundry in the eastus region
promptTemplates.scenario: Basic model deployment with preset configuration

promptTemplates.title: Custom Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy gpt-4 with Standard SKU, 100 TPM capacity, and content filtering enabled to westus2

promptTemplates.title: Find Capacity
promptTemplates.prompt: Find where I can deploy gpt-4o-mini with at least 50 TPM capacity

promptTemplates.title: Deployment Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the best regions for deploying gpt-4 with high capacity

bestPractices: Always verify capacity availability before deploying in production
Use descriptive deployment names for easier management
Configure RAI policies appropriate for your use case
Store deployment URLs securely for reference
antiPatterns: Do not use for listing existing deployments (use foundry_models_deployments_list)
Do not use for deleting resources (use dedicated Azure tools)
Do not assume all regions have equal capacity
Avoid deploying without checking availability first

faq.question: What Azure permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Azure Foundry contributor access or higher to deploy models.

faq.question: Can this skill delete existing deployments?
faq.answer: No, this skill is for deployment creation only. Use Azure portal or dedicated tools for management.

faq.question: How do I check deployment status?
faq.answer: The skill returns deployment status after creation. Use Azure portal or MCP tools for ongoing monitoring.

faq.question: What regions are supported?
faq.answer: All Azure regions that support Azure OpenAI service are supported.

faq.question: Can I deploy multiple models at once?
faq.answer: The skill handles one deployment request at a time. Repeat the command for multiple models.

faq.question: What happens if capacity is unavailable?
faq.answer: The skill will report unavailable regions. Use capacity discovery to find viable alternatives.
